anti_claim: Tool-server pressure inventory validates a public read-only helper-process pressure contract over synthetic process tables only. It does not read live host processes, signal processes, mutate host state, authorize release, call providers, export private account/session state, or claim whole-system correctness.

allowed_public_inputs: source refs, target refs, target digests, and anchor lists
negative case ids and stable error codes

standard_payload.anti_purpose: A host cleanup script, process kill list, live process inspector, release authority, or fixture-only regression wrapper that lacks source-module provenance.
failure_modes: active-owner descendant admitted as a safe-close candidate
unknown-owner or young detached process admitted as safe-close
over-budget active owner converted into a kill or terminate action instead of a release request
receipt or imported public body carries an absolute host path or live command body
source module manifest missing, target digest stale, or declared anchor absent
fixture result claims live process read, process signalling, host mutation, release authority, provider calls, private-data equivalence, or whole-system correctness
standard_payload.ontology: A public-safe accepted organ that ports the macro orphan_reaper read-only helper pressure classifier into synthetic fixtures with provenance-backed source-module body support.
standard_payload.teleology: Expose the real system's helper-process pressure hygiene membrane as runnable substrate without giving the public surface any host process-control authority.

contract_projection_basis.authority_boundary: computed standard-lattice contract only; existing authority_ceiling remains the operative claim ceiling.

validation_rules: Positive fixtures must classify detached safe-close candidates, active-owner descendants, unknown owners, and keep runtimes from injected synthetic process rows.
Active-owner descendants must never be safe-close candidates.
Over-budget active-owner groups must produce release requests, not process signals or termination results.
Exported runtime bundle must include source_module_manifest.json and a public-safe refactor body whose target hash and anchors validate.
Receipts must carry refs, hashes, counts, verdicts, and omission boundaries without embedding copied macro body text.
Absolute host paths, live command bodies, process-signal claims, release authority, provider-call authority, host mutation, private-data equivalence, and whole-system correctness claims are rejected.

author.purpose: create a valid Tool-server pressure inventory instance

refine_instance.purpose: improve an existing Tool-server pressure inventory without corrupting authority

refine_standard_and_propagate.purpose: change std_microcosm_tool_server_pressure_inventory and propagate through governed artifacts plus generated lattice projections

source_authority_ref: microcosm-substrate/AXIOMS.md#1 (JSON is contract; Markdown is projection)

doctrine_kind_envelope.note: This standard conforms to the universal doctrine-kind envelope; markdown, Mermaid, Atlas, coverage, health, and entry-card views are generated projections of this JSON source.

anti_claims: Tool-server pressure inventory validates a public read-only helper-process pressure contract over synthetic process tables only. It does not read live host processes, signal processes, mutate host state, authorize release, call providers, export private account/session state, or claim whole-system correctness.

Active v2 standard status is a source JSON contract state only; it does not expand release, publication, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, or whole-system correctness authority beyond the authority_ceiling.

runtime_acceptance_refs.uplift_basis: registry accepted_public_runtime_standard plus standard-local validator_contract, receipt_contract, source_refs, authority_ceiling, public_private_boundary, and anti-claim floor.
